content.problemStatement:
Finally, College has started calling students back to campus. There are so many students and thus due to some safety measures the college can’t call back all the students on the same day. It currently has the capacity of screening KKK students on a single day. There is a total of NNN students. What's the minimum number of days required for all the students to be back on the campus?


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N,KN, KN,K.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the minimum number of days required for all the students to be back on the campus.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: Since K=3K = 3K=3 and N=3N = 3N=3, we can call all 333 students back to campus on the first day itself. Hence it requires only 111 day for all students to be back on campus.
Test case 2: We have K=2K = 2K=2 and N=3>KN = 3 \gt KN=3>K, so we can't call all the students back to campus on a single day. But we can call 111 student on the first day and the remaining 222 students on the second day. Therefore, we require a minimum of 222 days for all students to be back on campus.
Test case 3: We have K=3K = 3K=3 and N=4>KN = 4 \gt KN=4>K, so we can't call all the students back to campus on a single day. But we can call 222 students on the first day and the remaining 222 students on the second day. Therefore, we require a minimum of 222 days for all students to be back on campus.
